volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 5.2.1167 - Section 5.2.1180b)

sections.label: Section 5.2.1167
sections.translation: Filth , tied up as you hang , you shall be beaten with stripes .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1168
sections.original: Interimam hercle ego te , si vivo .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I ’ ll be the death of you , if I live !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1168b
sections.translation: Make this pardon general ; do , pray , forgive Tranio this offence , for my sake .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1170
sections.original: Aliud quidvis impetrari perferam a me facilius ,
sections.translation: I would more readily put up with your obtaining any other request of me than that I should forbear sending to perdition this fellow for his most villanous doings .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1172b
sections.original: Istumne ut mittam ? viden ut astat furcifer ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1173
sections.translation: Tranio , do be quiet , if you are in your senses .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1173b
sections.translation: Only do you be quiet in urging this matter . I ’ ll subdue him with stripes , so that he shall be quiet .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1175b
sections.original: Áge iam , sine te hoc exorarier .
sections.translation: Come now , do allow yourself to be prevailed upon .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176
sections.translation: I wish you would not request me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176b
sections.translation: Troth now , I do entreat you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176c
sections.translation: I wish you would not request me , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176d
sections.translation: It ’ s in vain you wish me not ; only do grant this one pardon for his offence , pray , for my sake , I do entreat you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1178
sections.original: Quid gravaris ? quasi non cras iam commeream aliam noxiam :
sections.translation: Why make this difficulty ? As if to-morrow I shouldn ’ t be very soon committing some other fault ; then , both of them , both this one and that , you ’ ll be able to punish soundly .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1180
sections.translation: Do let me prevail upon you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1180b
sections.original: Age abi , abi impune . em huic habeto gratiam .
sections.translation: Well then , have it so ; begone , unpunished ! ( TRANIO jumps down from the altar .) There now , ( pointing to CALLIDAMATES ) return him thanks for it . ( Coming forward .) Spectators , this Play is finished ; grant us your applause [*] .
